The '''Nebulon-Q Swoop Racer''', also known as the '''Nebulon-Q racing swoop''', was a model [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] in the same [[Mobquet Swoops and Speeders/Legends|Mobquet]]-manufactured line as the [[Nebulon-R Racer]] and the [[Nebulon-S Racer]].

==Description==
The Nebulon-Q Swoop Racer was a single seat [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] bike manufactured by [[Mobquet Swoops and Speeders/Legends|Mobquet Swoops and Speeders]].<ref name="SWS" /> Measuring three [[Meter/Legends|Imperial standard meters]] long, the swoop could reach speeds of 600 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] per [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]],<ref name="SWS2" /> with its [[Ion engine/Legends|ion engines]]<ref name="UA" /> and had a flight ceiling of 350 kilometers. The Nebulon-Q had a cargo capacity of two [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] and was sold without weapons.<ref name="SWS" /> They sold for 5,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] new, and 1,050 credits used,<ref name="SWS2" /> and they were available for sale throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] although their sale was licensed.<ref name="UA" /> It was larger than the [[Nebulon-R Racer]].<ref name="Target" />

The craft was powered by Mobquet's [[AE318 swoop engine]] delivering thrust through two overcharged turbofans and featured a precise avionics suite.<ref name="Target" />

==History==
The Nebulon-Q Swoop Racer had a reputation for being a "muscle swoop", favored by [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]]<ref name="PP">''[[Pirates & Privateers]]''</ref> swoop gangs and delinquents<ref name="UA" /> throughout the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="PP" /> It was additionally marketed toward professional swoop racing teams.<ref name="Target" /> Built for speed, the Nebulon-Q contained minimum safety features including a safety strap that was rarely used and steering control surfaces. It was succeeded by the [[Flare-S swoop/Legends|Flare-S swoop]].<ref name="UA" />

The [[Dark Star Hellions/Legends|Dark Star Hellions]] utilized Nebulon-Q Swoop Racers,<ref name="SWS" /> as did [[The Skulls]], a swoop gang based in the [[Hook Nebula/Legends|Hook Nebula]], used modified Nebulon-Q swoops that could achieve speeds of 750 kmh, had a flight ceiling of 50 meters, and were armed with a light [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannon]].<ref name="GG9">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]''</ref>
